L1.area: Job scheduling configuration and data processing layer

L2.description: An XML external entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists where the XML parser used to process scheduling configuration data does not restrict external entity declarations or disable DOCTYPE processing. An attacker who can supply crafted XML input can include malicious DOCTYPE declarations with external entity references, enabling local file disclosure or server-side request forgery.

L3.area: XML-based scheduling data processing and document parser initialization
L3.description: An XML external entity (XXE) injection vulnerability exists where the document parser used to load XML-based scheduling data does not disable DOCTYPE declarations or restrict external entity resolution. A crafted XML scheduling configuration containing a DOCTYPE with external entity references can lead to local file disclosure or server-side request forgery when the scheduling data is loaded and parsed.

ground_truth.reason: The XML document parser used to process job scheduling configuration data does not disable DOCTYPE declarations or restrict external entity resolution, allowing XXE attacks via crafted job descriptions.
